title: Apartment Moving Tips from Experienced Movers
excerpt: Apartment moves: elevators, parking, neighbors, measurements, lobby protection, load order, noise rules, balconies, roommates, roles, deposits.
content:
Apartment moving tips from experienced movers

Apartments mean elevators, neighbors, tight turns, and limited parking—not driveways. Experienced crews succeed when building rules, timing, and labeling align.

Elevator reservations and buffers

Book service elevators when required; add ten minutes buffer. Confirm padding rules for cab walls.

Parking and staging legally

Know where the truck may stand without blocking fire lanes or diplomatic routes. Long legal carries beat tickets.

Notify neighbors and management

Post move windows; apologize for brief noise. Goodwill prevents complaints that halt work.

Measure the worst turn

Sofas fail at landings, not in living rooms. Measure clear width and height before moving week.

Floor and wall protection

Runners in lobbies; corner foam on banisters; masonite on marble when management requires it.

Load sequencing

Mattresses and essentials planned for first-off at destination if you need sleep same night.

Noise and drill curfews

Ask when power tools are allowed. Fines erase savings.

High-rise wind and balconies

Secure planters; line soil pots if they ride the truck; watch door wind gusts on upper floors.

Roommate and shared flats

Initials on boxes; one speaker for the crew lead.

Day-of roles

Door monitor while doors stay open; one adult for crew questions; another for kids or meals.

Deposit documentation

Photo walls before furniture shifts; clean after truck leaves if lease requires.

Service versus passenger elevators

Know which cab fits your tallest item. Split awkward pieces before the truck if disassembly is allowed.

Sound carries in shafts

Hammering at dawn may violate bylaws—confirm quiet hours.

Intercom and guest access

Test buzzer codes the day before; brief the crew lead on which unit to ring. Dead intercoms trap trucks curbside.

Split AC and wall units

Line-set moves may need licensed HVAC steps—book disconnection early if your lease requires professional removal.

Basement storage cages

Empty cages before load day; forgotten totes in cage storage delay final sweeps.

Conclusion

Apartment moves reward courtesy, measurement, and elevator math. Treat common areas as shared assets—crews will mirror your respect with careful handling.
author: Active Movers Team
